name: free-coding-models
description: Find, benchmark and install in CLI 158 FREE coding LLM models across 20 providers in real time
readme_summary: This JavaScript CLI tool helps developers find, benchmark, and install free coding-focused LLM models from over 20 providers. It features real-time parallel pinging to test model latency and a smart ranking system to highlight the fastest options, with additional features for proxy management and multi-account support.
key_features: Find, benchmark, and install free coding LLM models
Supports 159+ models across 20 providers (NVIDIA NIM, Groq, Hugging Face, etc.)
Real-time parallel pinging for latency testing
Smart ranking to highlight top 3 fastest models
Settings screen for API key management, provider enablement, proxy configuration
Multi-account proxy (`fcm-proxy`) for grouping accounts and auto-rotation
primary_use_case: To quickly identify and select the most performant free Large Language Models (LLMs) specifically for coding tasks, enabling developers to choose the best model for their needs based on real-time latency benchmarks.
cover_image_prompt: A dynamic scene depicting a skilled artisan, a 'Code Weaver', rapidly testing numerous colorful threads (representing LLM models) on a complex loom (representing coding tasks). Each thread's speed and quality are instantly visualized by glowing energy streams flowing from the loom. The fastest threads are highlighted with bright, golden light. Small, subtle UI elements showing latency numbers and provider logos are integrated into the background. The overall mood is one of efficiency and discovery, set in a bright, modern workshop. The art style should be a vibrant 3D isometric illustration with clear, bold outlines and a clean, futuristic aesthetic, emphasizing speed and choice.

name: git-mux
description: wip , a terminal UI dashboard for GitHub. Browse your repos, view commit history, PRs, issues, CI status, and contribution stats
readme_summary: Git-mux is a terminal UI dashboard built with Rust for browsing GitHub repositories. It allows users to view repository details, commit history, pull requests, issues, CI status, and contribution statistics directly within their terminal.
key_features: View commit history, PRs, issues, CI status
Stats panel with commit streak and languages
Repo detail view with comprehensive metrics
Start work: clone, branch, and open in tmux or locally
primary_use_case: To provide a comprehensive GitHub repository browsing and management experience within the terminal, enhancing developer productivity by reducing context switching.
cover_image_prompt: A bustling, organized digital workbench inside a cozy, dimly lit terminal window. At the center, a skilled artisan (representing the developer) is meticulously arranging and inspecting various glowing blueprints and schematics (representing GitHub repositories, PRs, issues, and commits). Small, helpful sprites or robotic arms (representing git-mux features) are sorting data, highlighting important metrics like stars and forks, and even preparing tools for a 'start work' action. Subtle code snippets and data visualizations float around the scene like ambient particles. The overall mood is one of efficient productivity and deep focus within the terminal environment. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic and a focus on clear visual hierarchy.

name: trends-checker
description: Python CLI for Google Trends analysis with advanced rate limiting, cookie auth, and   beautiful terminal output. Built on pytrends with enterprise features.
readme_summary: Trends Checker is a Python-based command-line interface (CLI) tool designed for analyzing Google Trends data. It offers advanced features like rate limiting, cookie authentication, and visually appealing terminal output, built upon the pytrends library to provide enterprise-grade trend analysis capabilities.
key_features: Multiple Search Categories (Web, YouTube, Images, News, Shopping)
Handling of Google Trends rate limiting (429 errors) via cookie authentication
primary_use_case: To analyze and visualize Google Trends data for market research, demand validation, trend tracking, and opportunity discovery through an easy-to-use CLI tool.
cover_image_prompt: A curious explorer standing at a crossroads, holding a compass that points towards glowing, interconnected lines representing global search trends. One path leads to a vibrant marketplace (representing shopping trends), another to a bustling newsstand (news trends), a third to a cinema screen (YouTube trends), and a fourth to a library (web trends). Digital particles representing search queries flow from the compass towards these destinations. Subtle UI elements showing charts and data visualizations are embedded in the explorer's backpack. The scene is set in a stylized, slightly futuristic landscape with a clean, modern aesthetic, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a bright, optimistic color palette.

name: Scrapling
description: 🕷️ An adaptive Web Scraping framework that handles everything from a single request to a full-scale crawl!
readme_summary: Scrapling is a Python framework designed for adaptive web scraping and crawling. It offers a flexible and robust solution for extracting data from websites, handling tasks ranging from single page requests to large-scale crawls.
key_features: Handles single requests to large crawls
primary_use_case: Automated data extraction from websites through flexible and scalable web scraping and crawling.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled librarian meticulously organizing and extracting information from a vast, interconnected library of digital books. The librarian uses a special magnifying glass that reveals hidden data points within the pages, which are then neatly cataloged into a flowing stream of organized information. Subtle glowing lines connect the books, representing the web. Small, translucent screens around the librarian display snippets of code and data visualizations. The repository name 'Scrapling' is subtly etched on a nearby desk. The scene is set in a modern, well-lit digital archive, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a clean, organized composition and a vibrant, tech-inspired color palette.

name: GeoAgent
description: [CVPR26] Official code for GeoAgent: Learning to Geolocate Everywhere with Reinforced Geographic Characteristic
readme_summary: GeoAgent is a vision-language model designed for image geolocation, capable of determining locations at various granularities (city, region, country, continent). It leverages a Qwen2.5-VL base and incorporates novel reward mechanisms for improved reasoning and accuracy, trained on a specialized dataset called GeoSeek.
key_features: Image geolocation at multiple geographic granularities
Geo-similarity reward for spatial and semantic matching
GeoSeek dataset for training and validation
primary_use_case: Accurately determining the geographic location of an image using visual and textual reasoning.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old cartographer, sitting at a large wooden desk cluttered with antique maps and a glowing globe, is meticulously examining a photograph. With a magnifying glass, they trace lines on a map, connecting visual clues from the photo (like distinctive architecture or natural landmarks) to a specific point on the globe. Faint, glowing lines of reasoning emanate from the photo, illustrating the process of deduction. Small, holographic projections of the image and its identified location (city, country) hover nearby. The overall mood is one of discovery and precision, with warm, focused lighting. The style is a detailed digital painting with a touch of magical realism, emphasizing the 'learning to geolocate everywhere' aspect.
